title: tephrosia aurantiaca subsp. rufopilosa
intro: Tephrosia aurantiaca subsp. rufopilosa is a plant species, commonly known as a legume or pea tree, belonging to the Fabaceae family. This plant has been traditionally used in various parts of the world for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimicrobial activities. The health benefits of Tephrosia aurantiaca subsp. rufopilosa include treating skin conditions, wounds, and infections, as well as reducing fever and relieving pain. Herbal preparations of this plant, such as infusions and tinctures, can be made by drying the leaves and roots, and then using them to create a medicinal tea or liquid extract.
